questions.text: Ethanol can be converted into ethanoic acid by:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Oxidation of alcohols convert them into aldehydes and ketones. The best reagent for this purpose is acid dichromate. Further oxidation of these aldehydes/ketones yields carboxylic acids.

questions.text: Which reaction is disproportionate reaction?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Aldehydes that have no a -hydrogen atoms undergo Cannizzaro’s reaction. It is a disproportionation (self oxidation-reduction) reaction. Two molecules of the aldehyde are involved, one molecule being converted into the corresponding alcohol (the reduced product) and the other into the acid in the salt form (the oxidation product).

questions.text: Which test is called silver mirror test?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Aldehydes form silver mirror with Tollen’s reagent (ammoniacal silver nitrate solution). Add Tollen’s reagent to an aldehyde solution in a test tube and warm. A silver mirror is formed on the inside of the test tube. High quality mirrors are manufactured by using this principle. Ketones do not give this test.

questions.text: Which of the following is not a fatty acid?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The aliphatic monocarboxylic acids are commonly called fatty acids because higher members of this series such as palmitic acid and stearic acid etc. are obtained by the hydrolysis of fats and oils. Phthalic acid is an aromatic carboxylic acid hence it is not classified as fatty acid.

questions.text: Which of the following is mono-saccharide:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Fructose is monosaccharide.Sucrose is a disaccharide that yields glucose and fructose on hydrolysis.Starch is a macromolecule or a polysaccharide. Cellulose is also a polysaccharide.

questions.text: Which three elements are needed for the healthy growth of plants?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The three elements most required by plants are nitrogen, phosphorous and potassium. Hence, fertilizers rich in these elements are utilised to boost plant growth.

questions.text: Ecosystem is a smaller unit of:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Ecosystem is a smaller unit of biosphere which consists of community of organisms and their interaction with environment i.e., animals, plants and microorganisms which lie in a definite zone and depend on the physical factors such as soil, water, and air.

questions.text: Which is the longest period of periodic table?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The periods 4 and 5 are called long periods. Each long period consists of eighteen elements. The period 6 is the longest period, which contains thirty-two elements. The period 7 is incomplete so far. It contains only two normal elements Fr and Ra, ten transition elements and fourteen inner transition elements.

questions.text: The word alkali is derived from which language?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The name alkali came from Arabic, which means ‘The Ashes’. The Arabs used this term for these metals because they found that the ashes of plants were composed chiefly of sodium and potassium.

questions.text: Which of the following is non metal?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Boron is the only non-metal in group IIIA. All other elements are considered metals. The small size and high nuclear charge make boron non-metallic while all the other elements of this group are metals.

questions.text: Which of the element gives acidic oxide?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Metal oxides are basic in character as they yield bases in water and non-metallic oxides are acidic because they form acids in water. There is a third type of oxides, which show both acidic and basic properties, these oxides are called amphoteric oxides. Hence, As, Sb and Bi give amphoteric oxides whereas Nitrogen being a non-metal gives acidic oxide.

questions.text: Which one of halogens is a liquid?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: .Fluorine and chlorine are gases of pale yellow and greenish yellow colours respectively at room temperature and pressure. Bromine is a liquid of red-brown colour and iodine is a metallic-appearing shiny greyish black solid.

questions.text: Total number of d-block elements are:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Total number of d block elements is 40.

questions.text: A double bond consists of:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: A double bond consists of one sigma and one pi bond.

questions.text: Formula of Marsh gas is:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Marsh gas, also known as methane, has the chemical formula CH4.

questions.text: Benzene cannot undergo:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The highly stable, delocalized electrons of benzene ring are not readily available for the nucleophillic attack like the electrons of alkenes. Therefore, the electrons of benzene ring do not assist in the attack of weak electrophiles. Hence benzene undergoes:electrophilic substitutionoxidationadditionIt does not undergo elimination reactions.

questions.text: For which mechanisms, the first step is the same?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: In E1 mechanism, like SN1 mechanism, the first step is the slow ionization of the substrate to give a carbocation. In the second step, the nucleophile attacks on hydrogen to give an alkene as a product
